title: Identity, Authentication, Sessions, and Recovery · Claim Defense
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for identity, authentication, sessions, and recovery and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for identity and session assurance model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.
Choose authentication and step-up strength from threat, consequence, usability, accessibility, recovery, and platform evidence.
Define issuance, binding, rotation, idle and absolute expiry, revocation, concurrent sessions, device evidence, and secure transport.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Identity Entity
steps.instruction:
Order the Identity Entity evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Identity Entity.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Identity Entity assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Identity Entity, keep Identity Entity connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Identity Entity has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Identity Entity, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Identity Entity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Identity Entity, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Account, person, credential, tenant, role, and session are conflated, and the accessible recovery path is weaker than ordinary sign-in. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate person, account, organization, service, device, credential, authenticator, session, and display identity.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Identity Entity, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One email address is a stable global person identity.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the quiz Identity Entity case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the quiz Identity Entity case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the quiz Identity Entity case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the quiz Identity Entity case

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Auth Failure Privacy

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Return usable authentication failures and telemetry without account enumeration, credential leakage, or inaccessible friction.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
